classes.name: ombott.server_adapters.GeventServer
documentation:
Untested. Options:

* `fast` (default: False) uses libevent's http server, but has some
  issues: No streaming, no pipelining, no SSL.
* See gevent.wsgi.WSGIServer() documentation for more options.

methods.name: ombott.router.parser.Parser._iter_parse
documentation:
valid examples:
    `/abc/:some/foo` - simple param (param name matches all from `:` to `/`)
    `/abc/<some>other/foo` - simple param (param name matches all from `:` to `/`)
    `/abc/<param.path()>baz/foo` - matches `/abc/a/b/cbaz/foo`, param is `a/b/c`
    `/abc/<param.rex((foo)|(bar)|(baz))[1]>/foo` - matches `/abc/foobaz/foo`, param is `foo`
    `/abc/<param.rex((foo)|(bar)|(baz))[2]>baz/foo`   - matches `/abc/barbaz/foo`, param is  `bar`
    `/abc/<rex((foo)|(bar)|(baz))[3]>baz/foo`   - matches `/abc/bazbaz/foo`

modules.name: ombott.router.radidict
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

variables.name: ombott.router.radidict.WEIGHT
documentation: doesn`t use at the moment

classes.name: ombott.router.radidict.MismatchType
documentation: mismatch types

methods.name: ombott.router.radidict.RadiDict._make_route
documentation: if route_pattern is sliced  param_exclusions, param_filters should be also sliced

methods.name: ombott.router.radidict.RadiDict._set
documentation: params = {name:str :  [is_exclusive:boolean, filter:callable]}

methods.name: ombott.router.radirouter.RadiRouter.__getitem__
documentation:
Return route object by the name or rule or pattern.

`key` treatment by type:
    `str` - name of the named route
    `set` - rule (must be single-item)
    `dict` - rule or pattern (must be single-item)

methods.name: ombott.router.radirouter.RadiRouter.remove
documentation:
use '*' to remove whole route-branch
remove('/foo/bar/*') - remove all routes starts with `/foo/bar/`
`/` - doesnt matter, so you can
remove('/foo/ba*') - remove all routes starts with `/foo/ba`

methods.name: ombott.router.radirouter.RadiRouter.resolve
ombott.router.radirouter.RadiRouter._match
documentation:
try to find the registered route, if filters is passed - performs filters comparision
_match('/foo/:bar:re([^/]+)/baz') - will parse route and retrieve filters
_match( 'foo//baz', [make_filter('re', '[^/]+')] ) - the same as above
_match(pattern, filters)  - is the same as -  _match(filters, route_pattern = pattern)
_match(route_pattern = '/foo//baz') - without filter comparision
    - used for removing, hook installation and general info

functions.name: ombott.ombott.abort
documentation: Aborts execution and causes a HTTP error.

functions.name: ombott.ombott.redirect
documentation:
Aborts execution and causes a 303 or 302 redirect, depending on
the HTTP protocol version.

classes.name: ombott.ombott._closeiter
documentation:
This only exists to be able to attach a .close method to iterators that
do not support attribute assignment (most of itertools).

methods.name: ombott.ombott.Ombott.run
documentation: Calls {func}`run` with the same parameters.

methods.name: ombott.ombott.Ombott.delete
documentation: overwrites by @with_method_shortcuts()

methods.name: ombott.ombott.Ombott.add_hook
documentation:
Attach a callback to a hook.

Three hooks are currently implemented:
    `before_request`
        Executed once before each request. The request context is
        available, but no routing has happened yet.
    `after_request`
        Executed once after each request regardless of its outcome.

methods.name: ombott.ombott.Ombott.error
documentation: Decorator: Register an output handler for a HTTP error code

methods.name: ombott.ombott.Ombott._cast
documentation:
Try to convert the parameter into something WSGI compatible and set
correct HTTP headers when possible.
Support: False, str, unicode, dict, HTTPResponse, HTTPError, file-like,
iterable of strings and iterable of unicodes

functions.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.cache_in
documentation: ------------------[ cachable property with a custom storage ] -------------------

classes.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.FormsDict
documentation:
This {class}`dict` subclass is used to store request form data.
Additionally to the normal dict-like item access methods,
this container also supports attribute-like access to its values.
Missing attributes default to `None`.

classes.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.CookieDict

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.CookieDict.decode
documentation:
Returns a copy with all keys and values de- or recoded to match
{attr}`input_encoding`. Some libraries (e.g. WTForms) want a
unicode dictionary.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.CookieDict.getunicode
documentation: Return the value as a unicode string, or the default.

This {class}`FormsDict` subclass is used to store request cookie data.
Additionally to the normal dict-like item access methods,
this container also supports attribute-like access to its values.
Attributes are automatically de- or recoded
to match {attr}`input_encoding` (default: 'utf8').
Missing attributes default to `None`.

classes.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.WSGIHeaderDict

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.WSGIHeaderDict._ekey
documentation: Translate header field name to CGI/WSGI environ key.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.WSGIHeaderDict.raw
documentation: Return the header value as is (may be bytes or unicode).

This dict-like class wraps a WSGI environ dict and provides convenient
access to HTTP_* fields. Keys and values are native strings
(2.x bytes or 3.x unicode) and keys are case-insensitive. If the WSGI
environment contains non-native string values, these are de- or encoded
using a lossless 'latin1' character set.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.FileUpload.__init__
documentation: Wrapper for file uploads.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.FileUpload.get_header
documentation: Return the value of a header within the multipart part.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.FileUpload.filename
documentation:
Name of the file on the client file system, but normalized to ensure
file system compatibility. An empty filename is returned as 'empty'.

Only ASCII letters, digits, dashes, underscores and dots are
allowed in the final filename. Accents are removed, if possible.
Whitespace is replaced by a single dash. Leading or tailing dots
or dashes are removed. The filename is limited to 255 characters.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.helpers.FileUpload.save
documentation:
Save file to disk or copy its content to an open file(-like) object.
If *destination* is a directory, {attr}`filename` is added to the
path. Existing files are not overwritten by default (IOError).

```
param destination

File path, directory or file(-like) object.

param overwrite

If True, replace existing files. (default: False)

param chunk_size

Bytes to read at a time. (default: 64kb)
```

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.request.BaseRequest.copy
documentation:
Return a new {class}`Request` with a shallow {attr}`environ` copy.

NOTE: \_\_listeners\_\_ are not copied

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.request.BaseRequest.__setitem__
documentation: Change an environ value and clear all caches that depend on it.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.request.BaseRequest.__getattr__
documentation: Search in self.environ for additional user defined attributes.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.body_mixin.BodyMixin.content_length
documentation:
The request body length as an integer. The client is responsible to
set this header. Otherwise, the real length of the body is unknown
and -1 is returned. In this case, {attr}`body` will be empty.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.body_mixin.BodyMixin.content_type
documentation: The Content-Type header as a lowercase-string (default: empty).

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.body_mixin.BodyMixin.chunked
documentation: True if Chunked transfer encoding was.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.body_mixin.BodyMixin.query
documentation:
The {attr}`query_string` parsed into a {class}`FormsDict`. These
values are sometimes called "URL arguments" or "GET parameters", but
not to be confused with "URL wildcards" as they are provided by the
{class}`Router`.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.body_mixin.BodyMixin.json
documentation:
If the `Content-Type` header is `application/json`, this
property holds the parsed content of the request body. Only requests
smaller than {attr}`MEMFILE_MAX` are processed to avoid memory
exhaustion.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.body_mixin.BodyMixin.POST
documentation:
The values of {attr}`forms` and {attr}`files` combined into a single
{class}`FormsDict`. Values are either strings (form values) or
instances of {class}`BytesIOProxy` (file uploads).

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.body_mixin.BodyMixin.forms
documentation:
Form values parsed from an `url-encoded` or `multipart/form-data`
encoded POST or PUT request body. The result is returned as a
{class}`FormsDict`. All keys and values are strings. File uploads
are stored separately in {attr}`files`.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.body_mixin.BodyMixin.files
documentation:
File uploads parsed from `multipart/form-data` encoded POST or PUT
request body. The values are instances of {class}`FileUpload`.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.body_mixin.BodyMixin._get_body_string
documentation:
read body until content-length or MEMFILE_MAX into a string. Raise
BodySizeError on requests that are to large.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.route
documentation: The ombott {class}`Route` object that matches this request.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.url_args
documentation: The arguments extracted from the URL.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.path
documentation:
The value of `PATH_INFO` with exactly one prefixed slash (to fix
broken clients and avoid the "empty path" edge case).

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.method
documentation: The `REQUEST_METHOD` value as an uppercase string.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.headers
documentation:
A {class}`WSGIHeaderDict` that provides case-insensitive access to
HTTP request headers.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.cookies
documentation:
Cookies parsed into a {class}`CookieDict`. Signed cookies are NOT
decoded. Use {meth}`get_cookie` if you expect signed cookies.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.get_cookie
documentation:
Return the content of a cookie. To read a `Signed Cookie`, the
`secret` must match the one used to create the cookie (see
{meth}`BaseResponse.set_cookie`). If anything goes wrong (missing
cookie or wrong signature), return a default value.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.params
documentation:
A {class}`FormsDict` with the combined values of {attr}`query` and
{attr}`forms`. File uploads are stored in {attr}`files`.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.url
documentation:
The full request URI including hostname and scheme. If your app
lives behind a reverse proxy or load balancer and you get confusing
results, make sure that the `X-Forwarded-Host` header is set
correctly.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.urlparts
documentation:
The {attr}`url` string as an {class}`urlparse.SplitResult` tuple.
The tuple contains (scheme, host, path, query_string and fragment),
but the fragment is always empty because it is not visible to the
server.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.fullpath
documentation: Request path including {attr}`script_name` (if present).

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.query_string
documentation:
The raw {attr}`query` part of the URL (everything in between `?`
and `#`) as a string.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.script_name
documentation:
The initial portion of the URL's `path` that was removed by a higher
level (server or routing middleware) before the application was
called. This script path is returned with leading and tailing
slashes.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.is_xhr
documentation:
True if the request was triggered by a XMLHttpRequest. This only
works with JavaScript libraries that support the `X-Requested-With`
header (most of the popular libraries do).

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.is_ajax
documentation: Alias for {attr}`is_xhr`. "Ajax" is not the right term.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.auth
documentation:
HTTP authentication data as a (user, password) tuple. This
implementation currently supports basic (not digest) authentication
only. If the authentication happened at a higher level (e.g. in the
front web-server or a middleware), the password field is None, but
the user field is looked up from the `REMOTE_USER` environ
variable. On any errors, None is returned.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.remote_route
documentation:
A list of all IPs that were involved in this request, starting with
the client IP and followed by zero or more proxies. This does only
work if all proxies support the ```X-Forwarded-For`` header. Note
that this information can be forged by malicious clients.

methods.name: ombott.request_pkg.props_mixin.PropsMixin.remote_addr
documentation:
The client IP as a string. Note that this information can be forged
by malicious clients.

functions.name: ombott.static_stream.get_first_range
documentation:
Return (start, end) of first range in ranges parsed from a HTTP Range header.
Skip unsatisfiable ranges. The end index is non-inclusive.

functions.name: ombott.static_stream._file_iter_range
documentation: Yield chunks from a range in a file. No chunk is bigger than maxread.

functions.name: ombott.static_stream.static_file
documentation:
Open a file in a safe way and return {exc}`HTTPResponse` with status
code 200, 305, 403 or 404. The `Content-Type`, `Content-Encoding`,
`Content-Length` and `Last-Modified` headers are set if possible.
Special support for `If-Modified-Since`, `Range` and `HEAD`
requests.

```
param filename

Name or path of the file to send.

param root

Root path for file lookups. Should be an absolute directory
path.

param mimetype

Defines the content-type header (default: guess from
file extension)

param download

If True, ask the browser to open a  dialog
instead of opening the file with the associated program. You can
specify a custom filename as a string. If not specified, the
original filename is used (default: False).

param charset

The charset to use for files with a text/*
mime-type. (default: UTF-8)
```

classes.name: ombott.response.BaseResponse

methods.name: ombott.response.BaseResponse.copy
documentation: Returns a copy of self.

methods.name: ombott.response.BaseResponse.status_line
documentation: The HTTP status line as a string (e.g. `404 Not Found`).

methods.name: ombott.response.BaseResponse.status_code
documentation: The HTTP status code as an integer (e.g. 404).

methods.name: ombott.response.BaseResponse.status
documentation:
A writeable property to change the HTTP response status.

It accepts either a numeric code (100-999) or a string with
a custom reason phrase (e.g. "404 Brain not found").
Both {data}`status_line` and {data}`status_code` are updated
accordingly. The return value is always a status string.

methods.name: ombott.response.BaseResponse.headerlist
documentation: WSGI conform list of (header, value) tuples.

methods.name: ombott.response.BaseResponse.charset
documentation: Return the charset specified in the content-type header (default: utf8).

methods.name: ombott.response.BaseResponse.set_cookie
documentation:
Create a new cookie or replace an old one. If the `secret` parameter is
set, create a `Signed Cookie` (described below).

```
param name

the name of the cookie.

param value

the value of the cookie.

param secret

a signature key required for signed cookies.
```

Additionally, this method accepts all RFC 2109 attributes that are
supported by {class}`cookie.Morsel`, including:

```
param max_age

maximum age in seconds. (default: None)

param expires

a datetime object or UNIX timestamp. (default: None)

param domain

the domain that is allowed to read the cookie.
(default: current domain)

param path

limits the cookie to a given path (default: current path)

param secure

limit the cookie to HTTPS connections (default: off).

param httponly

prevents client-side javascript to read this cookie
(default: off, requires Python 2.6 or newer).
```

If neither `expires` nor `max_age` is set (default), the cookie will
expire at the end of the browser session (as soon as the browser
window is closed).

Signed cookies may store any pickle-able object and are
cryptographically signed to prevent manipulation. Keep in mind that
cookies are limited to 4kb in most browsers.

Warning: Signed cookies are not encrypted (the client can still see
the content) and not copy-protected (the client can restore an old
cookie). The main intention is to make pickling and unpickling
save, not to store secret information at client side.

methods.name: ombott.response.BaseResponse.delete_cookie
documentation:
Delete a cookie. Be sure to use the same `domain` and `path`
settings as used to create the cookie.

Storage class for a response body as well as headers and cookies.

This class does support dict-like case-insensitive item-access to
headers, but is NOT a dict. Most notably, iterating over a response
yields parts of the body and not the headers.

```
param body

The response body as one of the supported types.

param status

Either an HTTP status code (e.g. 200) or a status line
including the reason phrase (e.g. '200 OK').

param headers

A dictionary or a list of name-value pairs.
```

Additional keyword arguments are added to the list of headers.
Underscores in the header name are replaced with dashes.

functions.name: ombott.common_helpers.parse_date
documentation: Parse rfc1123, rfc850 and asctime timestamps and return UTC epoch.

functions.name: ombott.common_helpers.tob
documentation: ---------------- [ Some helpers for string/byte handling ] ---------

functions.name: ombott.common_helpers.cookie_encode
documentation: Encode and sign a pickle-able object. Return a (byte) string

functions.name: ombott.common_helpers.cookie_decode
documentation: Verify and decode an encoded string. Return an object or None.

functions.name: ombott.common_helpers.html_escape
documentation: Escape HTML special characters `&<>` and quotes `'"`.

functions.name: ombott.common_helpers.proxy
documentation: ------------------[ exposes prop callable attrubutes at instance level] -------------------

classes.name: ombott.common_helpers.cached_property
documentation:
A property that is only computed once per instance and then replaces
itself with an ordinary attribute. Deleting the attribute resets the
property.

classes.name: ombott.common_helpers.NameSpace
documentation:
fast Name-Space-Dict
nsd.some       # - fast
nsd['some']    # - 20...30% slower
